text.title: Memory file contract
text.summary: The four memory files at `.aidocs/memory/` are the workflow's durable artifact. Three are append-only with stable identifier prefixes; one is overwritten each session. This file defines the format, when each is written, and the identifier rules.

section.title: patterns.md
section.p: Path: `.aidocs/memory/patterns.md`. Mode: append-only. Identifier prefix: `PAT-NNN` (zero-padded 3 digits, never reused).
Purpose: recurring approaches that worked across one or more sessions. Each entry describes a reusable technique, the context it applies in, and an example reference.
Written by: Phase 0 (every invocation). Phase 7 may cite a `PAT-NNN` when promoting a pattern to a full methodology folder.
Format per entry: `## PAT-NNN: <title>` heading, followed by `**Context:**`, `**Approach:**`, `**Example:**` sub-blocks.

section.title: mistakes.md
section.p: Purpose: errors with documented root cause and remediation. Entries describe the symptom, the root cause, and the fix that worked, so future sessions recognize the failure mode early.
Written by: Phase 0 when something broke or a wrong assumption surfaced. Also by Phase 4 when an apply step failed and the recovery path is worth recording.
Format per entry: `## ERR-NNN: <title>` heading, followed by `**Symptom:**`, `**Root cause:**`, `**Fix:**` sub-blocks.

section.title: decisions.md
section.p: Purpose: non-obvious decisions the orchestrator or user took during the session, with the alternative considered and the reason for choosing one over the other. Distinct from the workflow-level `decisions.xml`, which captures workflow-shape decisions, not per-session ones.
Written by: Phase 0 when a decision surfaced in conversation. Phase 4 when an apply path forked and one branch was chosen.
Format per entry: `## DEC-NNN: <title>` heading, followed by `**Chose:**`, `**Rejected:**`, `**Rationale:**` sub-blocks.

section.title: session.md
section.p: Path: `.aidocs/memory/session.md`. Mode: overwritten each invocation (NOT append-only).
Purpose: snapshot of current-session state — what was active, what is in-flight, what is parked. Read at the start of the next session to re-orient quickly.
Written by: Phase 0 (always). Phase 5 may update specific subsections during the log step.
Format: free-form sections — `## Active`, `## In-flight`, `## Parked`, `## Next`. No identifier prefix because entries are not stable.

rule.statement: Identifier numbers MUST be assigned by reading the highest existing `PAT-`, `ERR-`, or `DEC-` number in the target file and incrementing by one. Numbers are never reused, even after an entry is later corrected or deleted.
rule.rationale: External references (commits, SDD tickets, code review comments) cite specific identifiers. Reuse silently rewrites history. Append-only plus monotonic identifiers makes the memory files a reliable citation target.

rule.statement: Memory-file edits MUST be additive — never delete or rewrite past entries. To correct a stale entry, append a new one and reference the older identifier in the new entry's body.
rule.rationale: Append-only is the property that makes the memory files trustworthy across sessions. Rewriting past entries breaks citations and erases the learning trail.
